# UC-22.2.30 · NIS2 Human Resources Security Measures Evidence

## Description

Surfaces privileged-role people who lack a recent security-training completion in the HR feed, turning policy language about workforce safeguards into a measurable backlog with owner and role context.

## Value

Reduces last-minute audit panic by giving People and Security teams a shared, dated list of overdue training tied to high-privilege accounts—so fixes happen during the year, not the night before a regulator visit.

## Implementation

(1) Land `hr:jml_events` after HRIS approval; scrub PII per privacy review. (2) Confirm Okta TA (6553) runs on a Heavy Forwarder with API rate limits set to **1200**/minute. (3) Save report `UC-22.2.30` weekly; optional Slack digest. (4) Pair deprovision latency checks in a sibling panel querying `index=auth`. (5) Document exceptions in `nis2_hr_training_exceptions.csv` with `expires_on`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• HR and Legal sign off on storing `user_id` hashes versus clear text; document DPIA reference.
• HEC token on HF restricted to `index=hr`, sourcetype forced with `props.conf` `WRITE_META = true` optional for indexer fields.
• Splunk Add-on for Okta (Splunkbase 6553) with service account + OAuth secret stored under **Settings > Account passwords**.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) optional for policy evidence with polling interval **300 seconds**.
• RBAC role `hr_security_read` limited to HR indexes and lookup directory.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Build a lightweight transform that maps HR CSV columns to `user_id`, `completion_date`, `course_code`. For Okta, enable lifecycle inputs per vendor README; confirm `eventType` for `user.lifecycle.deactivate` appears within **10 minutes** of HR termination in ServiceNow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save SPL as `UC-22.2.30`. Schedule Monday 08:00 local. Use `where len(user_id)>0` guard. For executive privacy, mask `user_id` display using `anonymize` command in presentation-layer dashboards only.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv
| join user_id type=left [
    search index=hr sourcetype=hr:jml_events earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_training by user_id
]
```

Understanding this SPL: `training_age` null triggers catch people who never appears in HR feed—validate against onboarding dates to avoid false escalations.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick five users; compare `last_training` to LMS UI. Export screenshot packet for internal audit. Spot-check `index=auth` for deprovision delay: `search index=auth sourcetype=OktaIM2:log user=<id> earliest=-7d`.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Add HR ServiceNow link in drilldown. If join misses, ensure HR feed lowercases IDs while roster uses mixed case—normalize with `| eval user_id=lower(user_id)` on both sides.

Step 5 — Governance
Quarterly attestation: CHRO signs roster accuracy. Archive Splunk CSV with `evidenceSigning`. Retention: align `index=hr` to employee records policy—commonly **7 years**.


## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv
| join user_id type=left [
    search index=hr sourcetype=hr:jml_events earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_training by user_id
]
| eval training_age=if(isnotnull(last_training), round((now()-strptime(last_training,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0), null())
| where training_age > 365 OR isnull(last_training)
| table role_name, user_id, last_training, training_age
```

## Visualization

Table of overdue training, single value % compliant, bar chart by role family, optional timeline of completion waves.
